code.text: Specimen information

text.div:
Specimen information

Test08-MAY-2024Specimen
Appearance of UrineCloudy urineUrine
Odor of UrineUrine smell ammoniacalUrine
Clarity of UrineTranslucentUrine
Color of UrineBrown colorUrine

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and high urine protein and ketones, indicating possible nephropathy and ketosis. Urine is abnormal in appearance, color, and specific gravity, supporting renal involvement. Low HbA1c suggests possible anemia or recent hypoglycemia. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further renal assessment and clinical correlation for underlying causes, including dehydration, acute kidney injury, or chronic kidney disease.
